title: India condoles Khamenei's death; EAM Jaishankar speaks to Iran's FM
content: India has formally expressed condolences for the death of Iran's Supreme Leader, with the Foreign Secretary visiting the Iranian Embassy. Amidst escalating regional tensions, Prime Minister Modi has engaged in extensive diplomatic outreach, speaking with leaders of several Gulf nations and Israel. These discussions focused on regional security and the welfare of the large Indian diaspora.

title: 'No plans for rationing': How India is shoring up energy supplies amid Middle East crisis
content: Despite the rising tensions in West Asia, India remains confident in its energy supply, undertaking daily assessments and maintaining a strong inventory. By diversifying sourcing to include Australia and Canada, India enhances its energy security. In addition, strategic reserves and ongoing dialogues with global energy organizations and the United States reinforce India's readiness against any supply disruptions.

title: Google founder Sergey Brin reportedly linked to Billionaire Bunker
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin is reportedly linked to a $51 million waterfront mansion purchase in Miami's exclusive Allison Island. While his name isn't on the deed, transaction documents point to his involvement. This move places him alongside other tech titans like Larry Page and Mark Zuckerberg, who have also invested heavily in Miami's luxury real estate market, driving up prices.

title: Lasers, satellites and cyberattacks: How US is crippling Iran's arsenal
content: Although the fighting is taking place in the Middle East, much of the monitoring work is carried out thousands of kilometres away. Personnel in the United States operate from facilities housing large radar domes known as radomes. These spherical structures resemble giant golf balls and collect satellite data in real time. Analysts use the information to determine missile trajectories and likely targets.

title: Second Iranian warship heads towards Sri Lanka a day after US sub attack
content: A second Iranian warship is en route to Sri Lanka after a US submarine destroyed the IRIS Dena frigate, killing 87 sailors. Sri Lanka is discussing Iran's request for the second vessel to enter its waters for safety, with rescued sailors receiving treatment in Galle.

title: Chaos in Pak camp! Players unhappy with Hesson after T20 WC fiasco
content: Pakistan’s T20 World Cup Super 8 exit has triggered turmoil in the team after heavy losses to India and England. Reports claim several players complained to PCB officials about coach Mike Hesson’s strict and controlling approach during the tournament. The PCB is reviewing his conduct, but he may remain coach because he has a two-year contract and the board wants stability.

title: Mark Zuckerberg is done with Meta's highest-paid employee, this reorganisation proves
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg has quietly dismantled Alexandr Wang's power nine months after the $14 billion hire. The 28-year-old data labeling entrepreneur now oversees AI models being built on infrastructure he doesn't control. A new applied AI engineering organization under Andrew Bosworth routes talent and data pipelines around Wang entirely. Researchers report to other executives. Wang keeps his title but loses every lever that matters—a calculated pivot that suggests Zuckerberg's bet has already failed.

title: From Dhoni to Yuvraj: Cricket’s biggest names attend Arjun Tendulkar’s wedding
content: Indian cricket's biggest names assembled in Mumbai for Arjun Tendulkar's wedding. Stalwarts from various eras attended the grand celebration. Notable guests included MS Dhoni, Suresh Raina, Gautam Gambhir, and Jay Shah. Former coaches Ravi Shastri and Rahul Dravid were also present. Several former players and administrators marked their presence. Arjun married Saaniya Chandhok.

title: Indonesia gives Meta 'stern warning' about harmful content on FB, Insta and WhatsApp
content: Indonesia's Communications Ministry has issued a stern warning to Meta Platforms regarding its handling of harmful content, citing low action rates on reported issues like online gambling and misinformation. This warning follows a ministerial visit and highlights concerns over Meta's limited compliance with regulations.

title: With Amit Shah by his side, Bihar CM Nitish Kumar files nomination for Rajya Sabha
content: Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar filed his Rajya Sabha nomination today, accompanied by Union Home Minister Amit Shah. This move follows his decision to step down and head to Parliament, fulfilling a long-held aspiration. The transition, occurring after a recent election victory, signals a potential shift in Bihar's leadership within the NDA.

title: Trump's gold coup: Venezuela to ship a ton of bullion to US
content: Venezuela's state-owned mining company, Minerven, has agreed to sell up to 1,000 kilograms of gold dore bars to commodities trader Trafigura, with the gold destined for US markets. This multimillion-dollar deal, involving 98% final gold content, highlights strengthening US-Venezuela commercial ties under the Trump administration's supervision.

title: Draymond Green reacts to Blackpink K-Pop song mentioning his name
content: Golden State Warriors star Draymond Green expressed excitement after being name-dropped by the popular Korean girl group Blackpink. It was in their new song "Me and My." Green, a four-time NBA champion, views the K-Pop mention as a significant validation of his global influence and popularity. He plans to listen to the song with his daughters, who are fans of K-Pop.

title: Connor McDavid sends clear message as Oilers face key trade deadline
content: Connor McDavid quickly endorsed the Oilers’ trade for Connor Murphy, stressing the need to add talent without subtracting key pieces. With Edmonton firmly in a win now phase and draft picks holding less value, the move reflects urgency around the Stanley Cup chase. Chicago gains a future asset, while the Oilers strengthen their blue line for an immediate playoff push.

title: Gambhir attends Arjun's wedding hours before Ind vs Eng T20 WC SF - Watch
content: Indian head coach Gautam Gambhir attended Arjun Tendulkar's wedding in Mumbai just hours before the T20 World Cup semifinal. The ceremony, held at the team's hotel, saw a brief appearance by Gambhir and a few Indian squad members. Arjun Tendulkar married Saaniya Chandhok, daughter of a prominent businessman and granddaughter of Ravi Ghai.

title: Who killed Nancy Grewal? Family in Punjab alleges conspiracy after influencer’s murder in Canada
content: The mother of murdered Punjabi influencer Nancy Grewal alleges three individuals are responsible for her daughter's death in Canada. Grewal, known for her outspoken social media commentary on religious and political figures, had reportedly received threats and faced a prior house fire. Her mother believes a local gurdwara figure is the main conspirator.

title: Nifty50 ends above 24,750; Sensex up 900 points - top reasons for rally
content: The stock market staged a strong recovery as investor sentiment improved following reports that Iran’s Deputy Foreign Minister indicated the country was willing to give up its nuclear programme, among other developments that lifted market confidence.

title: What is your year-end tax readiness? Here's a checklist
content: India currently offers two parallel tax systems for individuals - Old Personal Tax Regime and New Personal Tax Regime. While the new regime is now the default option, the choice remains with the taxpayer.

title: Adin Ross’ Sister Madeline Ross Dies at 36 in Florida
content: Madeline Ross, sister of streamer Adin Ross, died on January 15 in Broward County, Florida, at age 36. The Broward County Medical Examiner confirmed the news to TMZ. The cause of death is still pending. Madeline lived a private life. Adin Ross remains one of the world’s most followed streamers despite past controversies.

title: What happens if India vs England semi-final washes out? ICC rules explained
content: India face England in the T20 World Cup semi-final on Thursday, with a reserve day in place by the ICC.  A minimum of 10 overs per side is needed for a result. If the match is entirely washed out, England, having topped their Super 8 group, would advance over India, who finished second in their Super 8 group.

title: What is Minuteman III missile? The 'doomsday' weapon US tested amid war with Iran
content: The United States conducted a test of its nuclear-capable Minuteman III intercontinental ballistic missile off the California coast. This unarmed missile launch, from Vandenberg Space Force Base, aimed to verify the effectiveness, readiness, and accuracy of the system. The test occurred amidst ongoing strikes in the Middle East.

title: Jaya Bachchan smiles wide as she attends Arjun Tendulkar's wedding
content: Arjun Tendulkar married Saaniya Chandhok on Thursday, March 5, with a star-studded guest list including cricketers and Bollywood celebrities. The grand wedding saw appearances from Amitabh and Jaya Bachchan, Shah Rukh Khan with his family, and legendary singer Asha Bhosle. The pre-wedding sangeet also featured a notable mix of sports and entertainment personalities.

title: 'Desi Maduro model, political abduction': Reactions pour in over Nitish's Rajya Sabha plan
content: Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar confirmed his Rajya Sabha nomination, sparking political uproar. Opposition leaders criticized the move, calling it a "leadership coup" and "political abduction" orchestrated by the BJP, betraying the people's mandate. Kumar assured his commitment to Bihar's development remains unchanged.

title: PCOS, thyroid problems & early heart disease rising in women: Experts explain reasons
content: The rise of lifestyle-related health concerns among women, such as PCOS, thyroid dysfunction, and premature heart disease, cannot be overlooked. These challenges often result from contemporary eating habits, heightened stress levels, and compromised metabolic health. Health professionals are advocating for women, particularly those in their 30s, to prioritize metabolic and cardiovascular screenings.

title: 'Games on black soil': Finn Allen reveals the secret behind NZ’s T20 WC surge
content: Finn Allen's record-breaking century powered New Zealand into the ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026 final. Allen credited their prior tour of India for adapting to the challenging black-soil pitches, enabling their dominant chase against South Africa.  He also lauded the bowlers for setting up the victory and praised his opening partner Tim Seifert's form.

title: Petrol and diesel prices: What does Middle East crisis mean for fuel cost? Check list
content: Despite escalating Middle East tensions and a surge in global crude oil prices, petrol and diesel rates in major Indian cities remain steady. Government sources indicate a strategy to shield consumers, with oil marketing companies absorbing some of the price hike. This approach aims to prevent significant inflation and GDP impact, ensuring stability for the public.

title: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander Injury Update: Will Thunder Star Play vs Knicks?
content: Oklahoma City's star guard Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's availability against the New York Knicks on March 4 is the key question. Dealing with an abdominal strain, his potential return could significantly impact the game. Despite injuries, the Thunder boast a strong 48-15 record, while the Knicks are 40-22 and formidable at home.

title: Chhattisgarh forest guard seen with woman companion thrashed, made to dance by mob
content: A forest guard in Surajpur was allegedly manhandled and forced to dance by local youths after being seen with a woman friend. Video clips of the incident, which occurred a week ago, have surfaced online, highlighting concerns about rising vigilantism. The forest department is awaiting the guard's formal response to initiate legal action.

title: 'My body has started sending me bills for my 20s': Redditor shares his wake-up call
content: A viral Reddit post reveals how neglecting health in one's 20s leads to physical "bills" in the 30s.  Young professionals are experiencing back pain, stiff necks, and aching joints due to poor sleep, bad posture, and improper exercise.  Experts confirm this accumulated damage is reversible with consistent effort and lifestyle changes.

title: ‘Nirantar’ Nitish turns a new page, set for Rajya Sabha after decades as Bihar CM
content: Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar is stepping down after leading the NDA to a significant victory, opting to move to the Rajya Sabha. This marks a major political shift for the veteran leader, opening the door for the BJP to potentially appoint its own chief minister in the state.

title: ‘Not a choke, a bloody walloping’: Coach’s brutal honesty after SA’s crushing loss
content: South Africa's T20 World Cup journey ended abruptly with a nine-wicket semifinal loss to New Zealand. Coach Shukri Conrad described the defeat as a 'bloody walloping' rather than a 'choke,' highlighting New Zealand's dominant performance. Finn Allen's record-breaking century sealed the emphatic win, leaving the Proteas stunned despite a proud tournament run.

title: Maruti 800 converted to Lamborghini! Jharkhand man's 'Jugaad' goes viral
content: A Jharkhand mechanic has transformed a Maruti 800 into a Lamborghini replica for approximately Rs 4 lakh. Mohammad Arif hand-crafted the modifications, including fiberglass panels, a wide stance, and a neon green finish. The custom build, featuring a two-seater interior and sunroof, has gained significant attention online.

title: Russian crude to rescue! Ships carrying Russia’s oil head to India amid supply shock
content: Ship-tracking data indicates that two Russian crude shipments which were originally bound for East Asia have redirected their course to India. With its energy security priorities in mind, India has sought to buy Russian crude to avoid supply shortages as Middle East tensions show no sign of abating.

title: Why did the boy who earned Rs 64 on a food delivery app is inspiring so many people?
content: A young Blinkit delivery worker, Manish Aswar, gained viral attention not for his earnings, but for his dignified approach to his first day. He shared his modest ₹65 income with pride and later celebrated his newfound online recognition by feeding stray dogs, highlighting the value of honest work.

title: West Asia conflict: 80 students among 111 Pune residents flown back from UAE
content: Over 100 Pune residents, including students, have safely returned from Dubai following the US-Israel-Iran conflict. A total of 184 were stranded, with efforts underway for the remaining travelers. Special flights arranged by Deputy Chief Minister Eknath Shinde also brought back 164 tourists from Maharashtra. Authorities are monitoring the situation and advising vigilance.

title: Who is Auston Matthews’ new girlfriend Vanessa?
content: Auston Matthews played a crucial role in Team USA’s gold medal victory at the 2026 Winter Olympics, scoring three goals and adding four assists during the tournament. After years of criticism in Toronto, the Leafs captain answered doubters on the international stage. Off the ice, Matthews confirmed his relationship with Toronto skincare specialist Vanessa Piunno, who publicly celebrated their romance on Instagram. With Olympic gold secured and personal stability emerging, Matthews appears to be entering a defining new chapter in both his career and life.

title: Man Dies By Suicide, Blames In-Laws For Mental Harassment
content: A man in Maharashtra has died by suicide. The man left a note in which he held his in-laws responsible for his death, accusing them of sustained harassment.

title: 3 Suspended Congress MLAs Join BJP Ahead Of Assam Assembly Elections
content: Ahead of the upcoming Assam Assembly elections, three suspended MLAs of the Indian National Congress, Kamalakhya Dey Purkayastha, Basanta Das, and Sashi Kanta Das, joined the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) at the party's state headquarters in Guwahati.

content: Azerbaijan said it was preparing to retaliate after two people were wounded on Thursday in Iranian drone hits on an airport and near a school.

content: As Nepal prepares for general elections next week, calls to restore the monarchy have also resurfaced across the country. The Shah dynasty, abolished in 2008, had ruled Nepal for 240 years before it was formally removed following mass protests.

title: 💍 Tennis star Sabalenka announces engagement
content: INDIAN WELLS, CA -- Top-ranked tennis player  Aryna Sabalenka  has announced her engagement to Brazilian businessman Georgios Frangulis. Sabalenka posted a video of the proposal on Instagram, accompanied by the words "You & me, forever," along with a ring and heart emoji. A post shared by Aryna Sabalenka (@arynasabalenka) The news quickly drew congratulations from fellow tennis players, including  Carlos Alcaraz ,  Novak Djokovic ,  Ben Shelton ,  Caroline Wozniacki ,  Coco Gauff  and  Amanda Anisimova , who is a possible opponent for Sabalenka in the quarterfinals at the forthcoming Indian Wells tournament in the Southern California desert. U.S. women's rugby player Ilona Maher also sent her congratulations. The tournament, which opens on Wednesday, will be Sabalenka's first since she reached the Australian Open final, where she lost to  Elena Rybakina  6-4, 4-6, 6-4 on Jan. 31.

content:
With Assembly elections approaching in Tamil Nadu, serious corruption allegations have surfaced in one of the State’s most important departments — Municipal Administration and Water Supply. The department is headed by senior DMK Minister K.N. Nehru. The matter has now reached the Madras High Court, which has ordered registration of an FIR.
Let us walk through what the Directorate of Enforcement (ED) claims to have found — and why the next steps could dramatically raise the stakes.

title: Conflict in West Asia hits Umrah pilgrims from Tiruchi district
content: Demand for Umrah bookings is high during the month of Ramzan (which began on February 19, 2026), with prices of 15-day pilgrimage packages ranging from ₹75,000 to ₹2 lakh per person

title: Amit Shah unveils mascots, soft launches four digital tools to power Census 2027
content: The mascots — 'Pragati' (female) and 'Vikas' (male) —  symbolise equal participation of women and men in fulfilling the resolve to make India a developed nation by 2047, a govt. statement said

title: Baramati plane crash: Maharashtra CID questions VSR Ventures owner
content: NCP (SP) MLA Rohit Pawar had alleged on March 4 that someone was trying to save VSR Ventures, and claimed that a preliminary probe by the AAIB vindicated the doubts earlier raised by him

title: Government investing ₹6,000 crore in substations to meet future power demand in Andhra: Minister Gottipati
content: The Minister said specifically in Vizianagaram district, two 220 kV power projects worth about ₹46 crore were presently under implementation

title: Revenue Department staff strike hits students, farmers: Anbumani
content: In a statement, he said around 42,000 officials and staff of the Revenue Department had been on strike since February 24, and that the government had not taken steps to resolve the issue

title: Nine out of 10 demands raised by anganwadi workers have already been addressed, says Minister Sandhya Rani
content: The 2025 budget has also allocated ₹20 crore to resolve their long-pending issues, the Minister told the Legislative Assembly

title: Union govt. trying to mislead people of T.N. over Tamil: Udhayanidhi Stalin
content: He said that in the past decade, the Union government had allocated ₹147 crore for the promotion of the Tamil language, compared to the ₹2,500 crore allocated for Sanskrit during the same period

title: Congress wants India to blindly side with Iran, even China distancing itself from Tehran: BJP
content: India's foreign policy, Amit Malviya added, must be "guided by national interest and the safety of its citizens, not by the compulsions of Congress' outdated ideological reflexes".

title: ‘Free’ vaccines, single-dose nudge pushes India-made HPV vaccine to back of the line
content: An ongoing study led by ICMR, which is testing whether a single dose of the India-made Cervavac vaccine generates enough protective antibodies and generates a stable immune response compared to a single dose Gardasil vaccine, will determine if Cervavac is officially recommended

title: Anand Patwardhan alleges he was blocked from meeting protesting monk at Mumbai University
content: ‘Why are they troubling a scholar of Pali who is on a dharna?’ filmmaker Anand Patwardhan asks after he was allegedly stopped from entering Mumbai University’s Kalina campus recently

title: PM Modi calls for swift end to West Asia, Ukraine conflicts
content: Mr. Modi made the remarks after holding wide-ranging talks with Finnish President Alexander Stubb, who is on a four-day visit to India

title: Karnataka ready to implement third phase of Upper Krishna irrigation project but BJP MPs are scared of approaching PM, says Minister M. B. Patil
content: Minister says BJP MPs from Karnataka are afraid to approach Prime Minister Narendra Modi with a request to issue a gazette notification to enable the State Government to go ahead with the work

title: NBA fast break: Who should you pick up before the fantasy playoffs begin?
content: the fantasy basketball playoffs are almost here, and context matters more than talent . teams chasing seeding are far more likely to lean heavily on their core players than those playing out the string . goal is not to build the flashiest roster, but to build most reliable one . prioritize players with secure roles and motivated teams .

title: India, China top Asia-Pacific region with highest metabolic diseases burden
content: In 2023, India had approximately 21 million DALYs and 5.8 lakh deaths from type 2 diabetes. High systolic BP caused nearly 3.8 crore DALYs and about 15.7 lakh deaths, Anoop Misra, one of the co-authors of the study said

title: Jyoti Waghmare: Shinde Sena’s pick for Rajya Sabha from Maharashtra
content: Ms. Waghmare joined the party in April 2023 following the Shiv Sena split, and was appointed as the State spokesperson and the district contact chief for Dharashiv

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election: CM Stalin expresses happiness over DMK-Congress seat-sharing deal
content: Participating in the marriage of School Education Minister, Anbil Mahesh Poyyamozhi’s relative at Thanjavur, Mr. Stalin said he had come after having successfully completing the seat-sharing deal which has been “appreciated by all”.

title: Iran-Israel war poses near-term challenges to Indian economy: RBI MPC member
content: “In the present scenario, a hike in oil prices, exports disruptions and impact on remittances have been identified as the immediate challenges,” RBI MPC member Nagesh Kumar said

title: Nitish Kumar’s political journey: From rebellion to longest-serving CM
content: Nitish Kumar has navigated the complex political terrain of Bihar, marked by strategic alliances and governance initiatives aimed at promoting inclusivity and development

title: India rejects claims of U.S. using its ports against Iran
content: The Ministry of External Affairs dismisses claims that the U.S. was using India’s ports to launch strikes against Iran, calling them ‘fake and false’

title: As Nitish Kumar eyes Rajya Sabha seat, political buzz over change in Bihar leadership mounts
content: All eyes are on Nishant Kumar, son of Bihar CM Nitish Kumar, amid indications that he may soon enter active politics

title: With exit of ‘forever CM’ Nitish Kumar, an era ends in Bihar
content: Nitish Kumar is part of a generation that ushered in the heydays of socialist politics in Bihar; with his exit as CM, the JD(U) is staring at a future in which it will be a junior parter to the BJP in the State

title: Rajya Sabha election: DMDK nominates treasurer L.K. Sudhish as candidate
content: The move will give the party founded by late actor Vijayakant its first representation in the Upper House

title: Curbs on movement, assembly remain in force in Kashmir after protests against Khamenei's killing
content: The restrictions were imposed on March 2, after spontaneous protests broke out across Kashmir against the killing of Iran's supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in U.S.-Israel joint strikes

title: Tamil Nadu restricts private schools from conducting political, ideological, communal events
content: The government has amended the Tamil Nadu Private Schools (Regulation) Rules, 2023, and has included clauses to restrict activities by any external person, association, or organisation

title: Comfort fit for conservancy workers: how three Indian cities are getting the ‘measurements’ right
content: What does it take to design gloves, masks and uniform that factor in the various challenges faced by workers on the waste beat? Indore, Bengaluru and Pune have an answer

title: Oxford’s Ashmolean Museum returns 16th century bronze sculpture of Thirumangai Alvar
content: The Ashmolean acquired the statue in good faith in 1967. According to the Sotheby’s catalogue, the bronze was sold by the private collector, J.R. Belmont (1886–1981).  There is no information on how the bronze entered his collection.

title: The SIR conundrum in West Bengal
content: While the Trinamool Congress under the leadership of Mamata Banerjee used SIR as an electoral opportunity and used the harassment of people to target the ECI and BJP led government at the Centre, the West Bengal BJP leadership appears to be disappointed with the outcome of the exercise as deletions in the electoral roll are far less than what they had wished for.

title: Excise detects rampant use of banned tobacco products by bus crew
content: The department booked over 30 cases under the Cigarettes and Other Tobacco Products Act during an intensive drive held in district

title: Internal reservation: Dalit Ministers hold meetings to arrive at a consensus ahead of Karnataka Cabinet meeting on March 5
content: Ahead of the Cabinet meeting during which the internal reservation issue is scheduled to be discussed, Dalit Ministers held parleys to arrive at a possible broader consensus on the issue that has now driven a wedge among Scheduled Castes and left the Cabinet divided

title: DMK-Congress deal: the leaders who were instrumental in breaking the stalemate

title: Tank of centuries-old temple in Thiruneermalai set to be restored by April, say officials
content: 50% of work on the tank at Sri Ranganathaperumal Temple near Chromepet over; concrete laying work and removal of silt under way, compound wall to be built, CCTV cameras to be installed

title: KGMCTA defers strike for a week following Kerala government assurances

title: Source: Pats to release Diggs after one season
content: the new England Patriots informed wide receiver Stefon Diggs that he will be released next week . the 32-year-old's salary cap charge was set to rise from $10.5 million to $26.5 million . if he was on the roster by the end of next week, he would have had an additional $6 million guaranteed .
